skill.name: ml-pipeline-workflow
skill.description: Build end-to-end MLOps pipelines from data preparation through model training, validation, and production deployment. Use when creating ML pipelines, implementing MLOps practices, or automating model workflows.
skill.summary: Complete end-to-end MLOps pipeline orchestration from data preparation through model deployment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 17 potential issues (external_commands and weak cryptographic patterns), all of which are false positives. The flagged backtick patterns are markdown code formatting for file path references (e.g., `resources/implementation-playbook.md`), not shell execution. The cryptographic algorithm flags do not correspond to actual cryptographic code in the skill file. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, no network access, no file system operations, and no security risks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Formatting
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at lines 22, 73, 81, 90, 103, 200, 211, 215, 219, 223. These are markdown code spans using backticks to format file paths like `resources/implementation-playbook.md` and `assets/`, not actual shell execution commands. This is standard markdown syntax with no security implications.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct examination of the file content confirms these are markdown inline code spans for path references, not executable shell commands.

content.value_statement: Building reliable ML pipelines requires orchestrating data preparation, training, validation, and deployment stages while maintaining reproducibility and monitoring. This skill provides proven patterns and best practices for creating end-to-end MLOps workflows that scale from prototype to production.
actual_capabilities: Design end-to-end ML pipeline architecture with proper stage dependencies and data flow
Implement DAG-based orchestration patterns using Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow, or Prefect
Configure data validation, feature engineering, and versioning workflows
Set up training job orchestration with experiment tracking integration
Design model validation frameworks with A/B testing and performance regression detection
Implement deployment strategies including canary releases and blue-green deployments
limitations: This skill provides guidance and patterns but does not execute pipeline code directly
Integration with specific orchestration tools requires separate setup and configuration
Cloud platform deployment specifics (AWS, GCP, Azure) require additional platform-specific knowledge
Performance optimization and distributed training patterns are referenced but not deeply detailed

use_cases.title: Build New ML Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a complete ML pipeline from scratch with proper orchestration, validation, and deployment stages for a production machine learning system.

use_cases.title: Orchestrate ML Workflows
use_cases.description: Design and implement DAG-based workflow automation for existing ML components using tools like Airflow, Dagster, or Kubeflow Pipelines.

use_cases.title: Deploy Models to Production
use_cases.description: Implement safe deployment strategies including canary releases, blue-green deployments, and automated rollback mechanisms for ML models.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a simple ML pipeline that processes data, trains a model, and deploys it. Include the key stages and dependencies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with your first ML pipeline

prompt_templates.title: Configure DAG Orchestration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a DAG-based ML workflow using [Airflow/Dagster/Kubeflow]. I have [data processing/training/validation] stages that need to run in sequence.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Deployment Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a deployment strategy for my ML model that includes canary testing and automated rollback. The model serves [description] predictions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning safe production deployment with risk mitigation

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Continuous Training
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a continuous training pipeline that retrains my model when data drift is detected. Include monitoring triggers and validation gates.

output_examples.input: Design a basic ML pipeline for image classification
output_examples.output: Here is a 4-stage pipeline architecture: Stage 1 - Data Ingestion (load images, split train/val/test), Stage 2 - Feature Engineering (augmentation, normalization), Stage 3 - Model Training (train CNN with hyperparameter logging), Stage 4 - Deployment (package model, deploy to serving endpoint). Each stage has independent retry logic and outputs versioned artifacts.

output_examples.input: How do I implement canary deployment for my model?
output_examples.output: Canary deployment strategy: 1) Deploy new model to 10% of traffic using weighted routing, 2) Monitor key metrics (latency, error rate, prediction quality) for 1-2 hours, 3) If metrics stay within thresholds, gradually increase to 50%, then 100%, 4) If anomalies detected, automatically rollback to previous model. Use MLflow or similar to track model versions and performance.

best_practices: Design each pipeline stage to be modular and independently testable for easier debugging and maintenance
Implement idempotent stages so re-running any part of the pipeline is safe and produces consistent results
Version all artifacts including datasets, models, and configurations for full reproducibility and rollback capabilities
anti_patterns: Avoid monolithic pipeline stages that combine multiple responsibilities, as this makes debugging difficult and reduces reusability
Do not skip data validation between stages, as invalid data can cause silent failures downstream that are hard to diagnose
Never deploy models directly to 100% production traffic without validation testing or gradual rollout mechanisms

faq.question: What orchestration tool should I use for my ML pipeline?
faq.answer: Choose based on your needs: Airflow for mature DAG workflows with large community support, Dagster for asset-based pipelines with strong data lineage, Kubeflow for Kubernetes-native ML workflows, or Prefect for modern Pythonic workflow automation. This skill supports all four options.

faq.question: How do I handle data versioning in my pipeline?
faq.answer: Use DVC (Data Version Control) or similar tools to track dataset versions alongside code. Create unique version identifiers for each processed dataset, and log these versions with your trained models for full lineage tracking.

faq.question: What is the difference between batch and real-time feature pipelines?
faq.answer: Batch pipelines process data on a schedule (e.g., daily) and are simpler to implement and debug. Real-time pipelines use stream processing to compute features on-demand for low-latency predictions. Start with batch, then add real-time features only if your use case requires sub-second prediction latency.

faq.question: How do I monitor model performance after deployment?
faq.answer: Track prediction latency, error rates, and business metrics. For prediction quality, use ground truth labels when available to compute accuracy/F1/MAE. Set up alerts for data drift detection and performance regression. Most teams use MLflow, Weights & Biases, or custom observability tools.

faq.question: When should I use canary vs blue-green deployment?
faq.answer: Use canary deployment to test new models on a small percentage of traffic while keeping the old model running. Use blue-green deployment when you need to switch traffic completely between versions. Canary is safer for model changes with uncertain performance impact; blue-green works well for infrastructure updates.

faq.question: How do I implement automated rollback triggers?
faq.answer: Define thresholds for key metrics (latency p95 < 100ms, error rate < 1%, prediction accuracy within 5% of baseline). Configure monitoring to check these metrics every 5-10 minutes after deployment. If any metric exceeds threshold for 2 consecutive checks, automatically revert to previous model version using your orchestration tool's rollback mechanism.
